abstract: The world is currently grappling with critical water challenges, ranging from pollution and ecosystem degradation to overextraction and inequitable access. To date, progress has been hampered by insufficient funding, ineffective implementation, and a common misdiagnosis of the root causes, which often prioritizes physical infrastructure over essential systemic changes like water reallocation. This paper re-evaluates global responses to the water crisis through a "Beyond Growth" lens. Informed by systems thinking, relationality, and justice, this framework seeks to decouple economic activity from environmental decline by overcoming policy inertia and fostering institutional reforms that prioritize the most disadvantaged.
